title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The tool itself does not pose an immediate security threat but could be leveraged by attackers to identify vulnerabilities in poorly secured systems if misused. The potential impact is low due to the absence of malicious intent and the open-source nature of the tool.
recommended_actions: Evaluate the tool's source code and functionality to ensure it aligns with organizational security policies before use.
Educate development and security teams on the proper use of vulnerability scanners to avoid exposing sensitive information.
Monitor community discussions and updates about the tool to stay informed about potential misuse or vulnerabilities in the tool itself.

title: A validation vulnerability has been identified in certain web features related to file management...
description: A validation vulnerability has been identified in certain web features related to file management or upload in several products of the TAO 2.0 suite. This vulnerability could allow an attacker capable of interacting with the affected feature to attempt to access file system resources outside the scope intended by the application.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to perform path traversal attacks, potentially accessing sensitive file system resources outside the intended scope of the application. This could lead to data exposure, unauthorized access, or compromise of the underlying server.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply any available patches or updates provided by the vendor for the TAO 2.0 suite to address CVE-2026-7185.
Implement strict input validation and sanitization on all file management and upload features to prevent path traversal attacks.
Conduct a security audit of the affected systems to identify any unauthorized access or exploitation attempts.

title: Imager::File::JPEG versions before 1.003 for Perl leak heap memory when reading a JPEG with...
description:
Imager::File::JPEG versions before 1.003 for Perl leak heap memory when reading a JPEG with repeated APP13 markers in i_readjpeg_wiol.

i_readjpeg_wiol walks the marker list libjpeg returns and, for each APP13 marker, allocates a new buffer with *iptc_itext = mymalloc(...) and overwrites the previous pointer without freeing it. Only the final payload is later turned into a Perl scalar and freed, so a JPEG with N such markers leaks the first N-1 payloads on every read.

In a long-lived process, s

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-13708.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-13698.

title: A flaw was found in GIMP's PNM file format parser. When parsing a specially crafted PNM file, the...
description: A flaw was found in GIMP's PNM file format parser. When parsing a specially crafted PNM file, the pnmscanner_gettoken() function writes a null terminator one byte past the end of a stack-allocated buffer due to an off-by-one error in the loop boundary check. This could lead to memory corruption, potentially resulting in denial of service or arbitrary code execution.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-58380.

title: Imager versions before 1.032 for Perl have a heap out-of-bounds read in the bundled Imager::File:...
description:
Imager versions before 1.032 for Perl have a heap out-of-bounds read in the bundled Imager::File::SGI reader via a 16-bit RLE literal run in read_rgb_16_rle.

read_rgb_16_rle guards each literal run with if (count > data_left), but count is a pixel count while every 16-bit sample consumes two bytes. The copy loop reads inp[0] * 256 + inp[1] and advances two bytes per pixel, so a run with data_left / 2 < count <= data_left passes the guard yet consumes 2 * count bytes and reads past the end of th

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Imager versions before 1.032 for Perl allows a heap out-of-bounds read, which could potentially lead to application crashes, data corruption, or exposure of sensitive data. This could be exploited by attackers to cause denial of service or gain unauthorized access to memory.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to Imager version 1.032 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Conduct a thorough review of applications using Imager to identify potential exposure and mitigate risks.
Implement input validation and boundary checks in applications to prevent exploitation of similar vulnerabilities.

title: A vulnerability was determined in GPAC up to 2.5-DEV. This vulnerability affects the function...
description: A vulnerability was determined in GPAC up to 2.5-DEV. This vulnerability affects the function gf_isom_nalu_sample_rewrite of the file src/isomedia/avc_ext.c of the component MP4Box. This manipulation of the argument nalu_out_bs causes double free. It is possible to launch the attack on the local host. The exploit has been publicly disclosed and may be utilized. Patch name: f29f955f2a3b5e8e507caad3e52319f961bf37bf. To fix this issue, it is recommended to deploy a patch.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in GPAC up to 2.5-DEV could allow an attacker to exploit a double-free issue in the MP4Box component, potentially leading to application crashes or arbitrary code execution. However, the attack requires local access, reducing its overall severity.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply the patch f29f955f2a3b5e8e507caad3e52319f961bf37bf to mitigate the vulnerability.
Conduct a review of systems using GPAC to identify and update affected versions.
Implement monitoring to detect any unusual activity that may indicate exploitation attempts.

title: A vulnerability was identified in GPAC up to b40ce70f5. This issue affects the function...
description: A vulnerability was identified in GPAC up to b40ce70f5. This issue affects the function sgpd_del_entry of the file src/isomedia/box_code_base.c of the component MP4Box. Such manipulation of the argument data leads to heap-based buffer overflow. Local access is required to approach this attack. The exploit is publicly available and might be used. The name of the patch is f29f955f2a3b5e8e507caad3e52319f961bf37bf. It is advisable to implement a patch to correct this issue.

title:
Untrusted Search Path vulnerability in B&R Industrial Automation GmbH APROL.

This issue affects...
description:
Untrusted Search Path vulnerability in B&R Industrial Automation GmbH APROL.

This issue affects APROL: before R 4.4-01P5.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The Untrusted Search Path vulnerability in B&R Industrial Automation GmbH APROL could allow attackers to execute arbitrary code by exploiting the software's improper handling of file paths. This can lead to unauthorized access, potential system compromise, and disruption of industrial processes.
recommended_actions: Upgrade APROL to version R 4.4-01P5 or later to mitigate the vulnerability.
Conduct a thorough review of systems using APROL to identify and remediate any unauthorized changes or suspicious activity.
Implement application whitelisting and restrict file execution paths to trusted directories only.

title: The software accepts user-supplied input via a URL parameter without adequate output encoding...
description:
The software accepts user-supplied input via a URL parameter without adequate output encoding before reflecting it back to the user's browser. This condition allows an attacker to inject malicious script content into pages served by the application.

By leveraging this weakness, an attacker can cause the user's browser to redirect to a malicious website, modify the UI of the webpage, or retrieve information from the browser. However, the impact is mitigated by the use of httpOnly flags on sessio

title: The throttling event handling mechanism in multiple WSO2 products accepts user-supplied JSON...
description:
The throttling event handling mechanism in multiple WSO2 products accepts user-supplied JSON payloads without sufficient validation of their structure and content. This allows an unauthenticated remote attacker to inject malicious JSON data that can lead to a persistent denial of service condition.

Successful exploitation of this vulnerability can disrupt the API Gateway, preventing legitimate API traffic from being processed and impacting complete service availability. The denial of service is

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the throttling event handling mechanism of WSO2 products allows unauthenticated attackers to inject malicious JSON payloads, leading to a persistent denial of service (DoS). This can disrupt the API Gateway, halting legitimate API traffic and causing significant service outages.
recommended_actions: Apply the latest security patches or updates provided by WSO2 to address CVE-2026-4249 immediately.
Implement strict input validation and sanitization mechanisms for JSON payloads at the API Gateway level to prevent malicious data injection.
Monitor API Gateway traffic for unusual patterns or spikes that could indicate exploitation attempts and deploy rate-limiting or throttling rules as necessary.

title: Apache Airflow's Google provider operators `GCSToSFTPOperator` and ...
description: Apache Airflow's Google provider operators `GCSToSFTPOperator` and `GCSTimeSpanFileTransformOperator` joined GCS object names returned by the bucket listing API directly to a destination filesystem path without normalisation or containment check. A user with write access to the source GCS bucket (typically a different trust principal than the DAG author — partner uploads, ingest-only service accounts, public-data buckets) could create an object whose name contains `..` segments and cause the DAG

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-49297.

title: An authenticated user could manipulate a company ID parameter in a POST request to the backend to...
description: An authenticated user could manipulate a company ID parameter in a POST request to the backend to gain unauthorised access to other companies hosted within the same subdomain environment. The application does not adequately verify whether the requested company ID belongs to the authenticated user’s session, resulting in a cross-tenant authorisation bypass. If this vulnerability is successfully exploited, it allows unauthorised access to sensitive customer information, including billing data, and

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated users to perform cross-tenant authorization bypass, potentially exposing sensitive customer information such as billing data. This could lead to data breaches, regulatory non-compliance, and reputational damage.
recommended_actions: Implement strict server-side validation to ensure that the company ID parameter in the POST request matches the authenticated user's session.
Conduct a thorough security audit of all authorization mechanisms to identify and fix similar vulnerabilities.
Deploy monitoring and logging to detect and alert on unusual access patterns or unauthorized access attempts.

title: Generation of Error Message Containing Sensitive Information vulnerability in Apache Camel...
description:
Generation of Error Message Containing Sensitive Information vulnerability in Apache Camel Undertow Component.

The camel-undertow HTTP server consumer exposes a muteException option that controls what is returned to the client when a route processing error occurs. This option defaulted to false, whereas the other Camel HTTP server components (camel-http / camel-jetty / camel-servlet and camel-platform-http) default it to true. With muteException=false, when a request triggers an exception durin

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows sensitive information to be exposed in error messages when the muteException option is set to false in the camel-undertow HTTP server consumer. This could lead to information disclosure, aiding attackers in reconnaissance or exploitation.
recommended_actions: Immediately update Apache Camel to the latest version that addresses CVE-2026-56139.
Review and set the muteException option to true in the camel-undertow HTTP server consumer configuration to prevent sensitive information exposure.
Conduct a thorough audit of server logs and error messages to ensure no sensitive data has already been leaked.

title: A flaw was found in Red Hat Advanced Cluster Security for Kubernetes (RHACS). Central does not...
description: A flaw was found in Red Hat Advanced Cluster Security for Kubernetes (RHACS). Central does not limit the depth of GraphQL queries served on the authenticated GraphQL API. An authenticated user with a valid API token can send deeply nested queries that cause excessive resource consumption in Central, resulting in a denial of service for the management plane.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-9165.

title: Improper Authentication, Missing Authentication for Critical Function, Not Failing Securely (...
description:
Improper Authentication, Missing Authentication for Critical Function, Not Failing Securely ('Failing Open') vulnerability in Apache Camel Keycloak Component.

The KeycloakSecurityPolicy of camel-keycloak guards a route by running KeycloakSecurityProcessor.beforeProcess(), which performs three checks in sequence: it rejects a request that carries no access token, then - only if requiredRoles is non-empty - validates the roles, and - only if requiredPermissions is non-empty - validates the permis

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Apache Camel Keycloak Component could allow unauthorized access to critical functions or sensitive data due to improper authentication mechanisms and failure to securely reject invalid requests ('Failing Open'). This may lead to privilege escalation or data breaches.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to the latest patched version of Apache Camel Keycloak Component as soon as it becomes available.
Implement additional authentication and authorization checks at the application level to mitigate risks until the patch is applied.
Conduct a thorough security audit of systems using the affected component to identify potential exploitation.

title: Improper Input Validation, Improper Neutralization of Special Elements in Output Used by a...
description:
Improper Input Validation, Improper Neutralization of Special Elements in Output Used by a Downstream Component ('Injection') vulnerability in Apache Camel IRC component.

The camel-irc producer chooses the destination of an outgoing IRC message from the irc.sendTo Exchange header (the constant IrcConstants.IRC_SEND_TO, value irc.sendTo); when that header is present it overrides the channel list configured on the endpoint, and the message is sent only to the specified destination. This and the c

title: CVE-2026-13527 - A vulnerability has been found in SourceCodester Class and Exam Timetabling System 1.0. The affected
description: A vulnerability has been found in SourceCodester Class and Exam Timetabling System 1.0. The affected element is an unknown function of the file /preview4.php. Such manipulation of the argument course_year_section leads to sql injection. The attack may be launched remotely. The exploit has been disclosed to the public and may be used.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to perform SQL injection via the course_year_section parameter in the /preview4.php file, potentially leading to unauthorized access, data leakage, or database manipulation.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply input validation and sanitization on the course_year_section parameter to prevent SQL injection.
Update or patch the Class and Exam Timetabling System to the latest version if a fix is available.
Monitor server logs for unusual activity, especially targeting /preview4.php, and implement Web Application Firewall (WAF) rules to block malicious requests.

description: A security vulnerability has been detected in CherryHQ cherry-studio up to 1.9.6. title: CVE-2026-13523 - A weakness has been identified in GPAC up to 26.02.0. This affects an unknown part of the file src/u
description: A weakness has been identified in GPAC up to 26.02.0. This affects an unknown part of the file src/utils/base_encoding.c of the component ISOBMFF Parser. Executing a manipulation can lead to highly compressed data. The attack needs to be launched locally. The exploit has been made available to the public and could be used for attacks. This patch is called 297f2d8d1f493d8b241330533cd47f7da758aeb3. A patch should be applied to remediate this issue. The vendor confirms: "We added a check on inflate output size, if it surpasses 32 times the input size we stop in error. This value could be adjusted later."

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2026-13523.

title: CVE-2026-13514 - A weakness has been identified in Chess Play and Learn App up to 4.9.42 on Android. This issue affec
description: A weakness has been identified in Chess Play and Learn App up to 4.9.42 on Android. This issue affects some unknown processing of the file AndroidManifest.xml of the component com.chess. This manipulation causes exposure of backup file to an unauthorized control sphere. It is feasible to perform the attack on the physical device. The exploit has been made available to the public and could be used for attacks. Upgrading the affected component is advised. The vendor was informed early about this issue. They confirmed the existence and that they will address it. Furthermore, they explain that their bug bounty "explicitly excludes physical-access attacks". However, they appreciate the quality of the report and aim at making a goodwill payment to the researcher.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2026-13514.

description: A security flaw has been discovered in MyScale MyScaleDB up to 1.8.0. This vulnerability affects the function SegmentId::getCacheKey in the library src/VectorIndex/Common/SegmentId.h.
